name: accordion
description: A vertically stacked set of collapsible content sections. Built on Radix UI Accordion.

props.name: type
props.description: Single allows one item open at a time, multiple allows many

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Default open item(s): string for type='single', string[] for type='multiple'

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled open item(s): string for type='single', string[] for type='multiple'

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Callback when open items change: (value: string) => void for single, (value: string[]) => void for multiple

props.name: collapsible
props.description: Allow all items to be closed (type='single' only)

examples.title: FAQ accordion
examples.code:
<Accordion type="single" collapsible>
  <AccordionItem value="item-1">
    <AccordionTrigger>Is it accessible?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>Yes, it adheres to the WAI-ARIA design pattern.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem value="item-2">
    <AccordionTrigger>Is it styled?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>Yes, with Tailwind CSS and smooth animations.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

ai.whenToUse: Use for FAQ sections, settings groups, or any content that benefits from progressive disclosure. Use type='single' for FAQs, type='multiple' for settings.
ai.whenNotToUse: Don't use for navigation (use tabs). Don't use for a single collapsible (use Collapsible).
commonMistakes: Not setting collapsible=true for single type when all items should be closeable
ai.accessibilityNotes: Full keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Home, End, Enter/Space). Radix handles aria-expanded, aria-controls, role='region'.
